sol: In Eastern India (Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa) during the Pala Kingdom in the ninth and tenth centuries A.D., miniature painting was established. With a long history that extends back to the scribes of the mediaeval periods, miniature painting is a genre that concentrates on art (particularly painting, engraving, and sculpture). Miniature paintings, sketches, original prints and etchings, sculptures, and more can be found in collections at the American White House, the Smithsonian American Art Museum, Astolat Dollhouse Castle, and museums all around the world. During the Late Classical era, the Indian subcontinent was ruled by the Pala Empire. Any work of art that is painted or applied directly to a wall, ceiling, or other durable surfaces is referred to as a mural painting. The town of Thanjavur (also known as Tanjore in English) is where the classical South Indian painting style known as Thanjavur painting first emerged. Oil painting is the practice of using pigments and a drying oil substance as a binder.

It was an undersea megathrust earthquake that measured 9.1–9.3 Mw and in some places reached a Mercalli rating of IX. An earthquake occurred as a result of a breach along the fault line separating the Indian and Burmese plates. The faulting lasted the longest time ever seen, between eight and ten minutes, and the earthquake was the third- largest ever recorded. The earthquake that occurred is referred to as the Sumatra-Andaman earthquake by scientists. The subsurface seismic activity resulted in a succession of huge tsunami waves that reached heights of up to 30 metres (100 feet).

The students are going to be taken on a picnic by the teacher.
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error.
The children in the colony fed the dogs by throwing pieces of meat on them.
sol: The Ministry of Drinking Water and Sanitation presented the awards. According to the Swachh Survekshan Grameen 2018 rankings, Satara District in Maharashtra was named the best district, and Haryana was named the best State. Gujarat won a prize for having the second-highest level of citizen participation.

To change an active voice into passive voice, first the interchange of subjects and objects take place. The given sentence has the verb in simple past tense, so in passive voice the verb becomes : Object + was/were + Verb [past participle] + by/to /with + Subject. Therefore, correct answer is : The application form was given to me by the secretary.

q: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom. sail close to the wind
opts: do something that is dangerous or even illegal
make attempts to improve a difficult situation
do something that is legal and acceptable
sail one’s boat close to the shore
sol: The idea of Black Friday being observed as the beginning of the Christmas shopping season makes ‘regarded’ (consider or think of in a specified way) the perfect option. Option (3) and (4) are more subjective and personal, thus ruled out.

q: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. ruined
sol: Since Black Friday is observed as the beginning of the Christmas shopping season, major / main retailers open early. The appropriateness of ‘influential / considerable / important’ retailers is highly questionable.

q: Read the following passing and answer the question. I was so homesick during my first two weeks at St. Peter’s that I devised a way of getting myself sent back home. My idea was that I should all of a sudden develop an attack of acute appendicitis.
opts: When I reported my sickness to the teacher, I was sent to the Matron. I entered the room clutching my stomach on the right- hand side and staggering pathetically.
‘What’s the matter with you boy?’ the Matron shouted.
‘It hurts, Matron,’ I moaned. ‘Oh, it hurts so much! Just here!’ ‘You’ve been over-eating!’ she braked.
‘I haven’t eaten a thing for days,’ I lied.
sol: Retailers offering sales is better than saying retailers ‘making available/coming up with/putting forward’ promotional sales.

q: She began prodding my tummy violently with her figures. I was watching her carefully, and when she hit what I guessed was the appendix place, I let out a yelp that rattled the window- panes. ‘Ow! Ow! Ow!’ I cried out. ‘Don’t Matron, don’t!’ Then I slipped in the clincher. ‘I’ve been sick all morning.’ I moaned. This was the right move. Within an hour, the doctor arrived and as he examined me, I did m yelping at what I thought were the proper times. This Matron returned half an hour later and said, ‘Your mother’s coming to fetch you this afternoon.’ I didn’t answer her. I just lay there trying to look very ill, but my heart was singing all sorts of wonderful songs. As I was taken home on a ferry, I felt so wonderful to leave that dreaded school building that I very nearly forgot I was meant to be ill. The Matron suspected that the boy had appendicitis because: He informed his teacher that he felt unwell. he hadn’t been able to eat anything for days. he staggered into the room clutching his stomach. he screamed when she pressed the right side of his stomach. The boy had been at St. Peter’s for:
sol: Retailers offering promotional sales ‘kick off’ the holiday shopping season. ‘Kick off’ implies to become full of action and excitement; ‘set off’ means ‘begin a journey.’

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. Dan said, “I need a clean shirt.”
opts: Dan said that he will need a clean shirt
Dan said that he would need a clean shirt
Dan said that he needed a clean shirt
Dan said that he needs a clean shirt
sol: The next blank sets in the idea that with the three days following Thanksgiving, it increases the prospect of potential buyers, who are likelier to have gone to their favourite retailers. The adverb ‘likely’ cannot be applied before people.

q: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. conceited
sol: Black Friday has been reputed to be the busiest shopping day of the year since 2005; this implies that it has been consistently/constantly been hailed as the busiest till today.

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. “Children are eating the wrong foods,” remarked Mrs. Nair
opts: Mrs. Nair remarked that children ate the wrong foods.
Mrs. Nair remarked that children are eating the wrong foods.
Mrs. Nair remarked that children eat the wrong foods.
Mrs. Nair remarked that children have been eating the wrong foods.
sol: The sentence claims that the first proof of the phrase ‘Black Friday’ in the context of shopping, relates to its origin. So ‘evidence’ is the correct choice.

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. “I don’t want to hang this picture here,” Mary said.
opts: Mary said she didn’t want to hang this picture here.
Mary said she didn’t want to hang that picture here.
Mary said she didn’t want to hang this picture there.
Mary said she didn’t want to hang that picture there.
sol: Context refers to the background of the event or the wider situation. Here, the wider situation is the relation/origin of shopping with Black Friday. So the use of the phrase Black Friday in a shopping context suggests that it originated in Philadelphia.

q: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. vibrant
sol: The phrase ‘heavy traffic’ connotes that it must have been a disruptive one (causing disruption). Traffic cannot be attributed as ‘disturbing’ (upsetting)/‘meddling’ (intrusive)/interfering’.

q: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Our forces had conquered the fort by day-break The fort was being conquered by our forces before day- break. The fort will be conquered by our forces before day-break. The fort had been conquered by our forces before day- break. The fort was conquered by our forces before day-break. Given below are four sentence, three of which, are jumbled pick the option that gives the correct order.
opts: As the train approached the seaside town, I went into the corridor to stretch legs.
I had met him earlier on the platform.
I stayed there for a short while.
I exchanged a few words with one of the passengers.
sol: An event can be said to ‘date’ back to a certain day/date. Date back to something means to be made or begun at a particular time in the past.
They have done lot of research before making the film. It is to be noted that ‘research’ is already a plural noun, so it need not have an ‘s/es’ after it. Therefore ‘a lot of research’ is correct.

q: 171 Given below are four sentences, three of which, are jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order. In the hotel room I rested till dinner time. I had not escaped from my tiresome neighbour after all. I had barely raised the glass to my lips when a familiar voice greeted me. Then I went down and ordered a drink.
sol: Whenever I went to the cinema, I bought myself popcorn and coke as a special treat. The preposition ‘for’ will be replaced by ‘as’; the latter is used to refer to any object’s function.

q: Select the alternative that will improve the underlined part of the sentence. In case there is no improvement select “No improvement”. Rohit is thinking to buy himself a new car.
opts: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error.
We were woke up in the middle of the night by the sound of bursting crackers.
sol: Can anyone tell me what we learnt in Mathematics yesterday? The sentence has an incorrect usage of ‘did’. It will take simple past/past participle tense. This is so because the sentence has a past phrase of time ‘yesterday’.

Jayan woke up having a sore throat and by evening his voice had disappeared. ‘Have’ is replaced by ‘having’ because the action is a continuous one- after waking up he was still having a sore throat…’.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The fire fighters have finally managed to get the forest fire . out of control 2. with control 3. under control 4. in control Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I would appreciate it if you do not get into any with your colleagues.
sol: I think boys and girls must learn to do household chores from an early age; ‘be learning’ is incorrect. The sentence presents an opinion in simple present tense. So ‘must learn’ is appropriate.

q: Read the following passage and answer the questions. The Chinese have been drinking tea for health and enjoyment for more than 5000 years. No one knows what drew them to the glossy green leaves of Camellia sinensis, but a popular lengend fills the gap in our knowledge. According to legend, ShenNong, an early emperor required that all drinking water be boiled. One summer day while visiting a distant region of his kingdom, he stopped to rest because he felt very tried. The servants began to boil water for the king and his ministers to drink. Dried leaves from a nearby bush fell into the boiling water, turning it into a brown liquid. The Emperor drank some of the new liquid. He felt fresh and much better after drinking the liquid. And thus, tea was created.
opts: According to an Indian legend a Buddhist monk, on one of his travels about 2000 years ago, swore not to sleep during the nine years of his journey. However, by the end of the third year, he was so fatigued he almost fell asleep. That is when he accidentally stumbled upon a wild tree and picked a few leaves from its top. He chewed on the leaves and soon recovered. Thanks to these leaves, he was able to stay awake during the next six years of his mission. The Japanese version of this legend is slightly different. The monk had vowed to meditate for 7 years without sleep. One night however, he fell asleep. He was so angry with himself that he cut off his eyelids and threw them to the ground. A few years later, passing by the same spot, he noticed a strange-looking bush. He tasted its leaves, and realized that they gave him the power to keep his eyes open. This story soon spread, and tea bushes are often seen in Buddhist places of worship.
In all the three legends, the leaves help the person who eat it to recover. This suggests that the leaves:
are endowed with some magical powers
can refresh and rejuvenate a person
sol: Those students who are late will not be allowed to enter the examination hall. Here, distributive pronoun ‘those’ will be used to refer to students who are late, instead of indefinite pronoun ‘some’.

q: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom. twist someone’s arm
opts: to force someone to do something by making it hard for them to refuse
to make someone who is annoyed even angrier
to persuade someone to do something you want because they like you
to hurt someone who is injured by twisting their arm
sol: Minu thinks that Salman is a better singer than Nitin. Since comparison is made between two people – Salman and Nitin, the adjective in the comparative degree must be used.

q: Select the alternative that will improve the underlined part of the sentence. In case there is no improvement select “No improvement”. The Western disturbances blowing towards North India are colder as before. are cold as 2. more colder than 3. No improvement 4. are colder than Given below are four sentences, three of which, are jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order. Is there a chemist nearby?
opts: You’ll find a big chemist shop a hundred meters up the road
Yes Madam, it’s about three minutes walk from here.
Take the first right turn, then take the second turn to the left.
sol: For voice change, first the interchange of subjects and objects take place. The given sentence has the verb in simple past tense, so in passive voice the verb becomes : Object + was/were + Verb [past participle] + by/to/with + Subject . The correct sentence is: The grades were not submitted by Mr. Zabihullah on time.

The correct sequence is: ADCB. D gives a clue- ‘before I answer your question’. This signifies that a question has already been posed before the second person. Thus, A precedes D with: You do buy old books, don’t you? The statement - Business is not so good as it used to be gets its validation, in B. the reason behind business not doing well is people seem to prefer to buy new books nowadays.

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. Alice enquired, “At what time is the taxi coming?” Alice enquired at what time the taxi was coming Alice enquired at what time the taxi is coming.
opts: Alice enquired at what time the taxi would come.
Alice enquired at what time the taxi has been coming.
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error.
My nephew has been in hospital since four weeks because of mumps.
sol: The correct sequence is: ABDC. The pronoun ‘he’ in B has its antecedent in A (a man who was my neighbour years ago). So AB is a convincing pair. Since this man was a great talker, it used to take hours to get away from him. So logically ABD pair up.

The sentence ‘I arrived in India last week,” said Sarah’ when changed to indirect speech, will incorporate changes like: simple past tense ‘arrived’ changes to past perfect ‘had arrived’; last week changes to the previous week.

q: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Raju sells flowers in the market every Sunday.
opts: Every Sunday, flowers are sold in the market by Raju.
Every Sunday, flowers are being sold, in the market by Raju.
Every Sunday, flowers were sold in the market by Raju.
Every Sunday, Raju is sold by flowers in the market.
sol: Anshita and I are going to the mall. ‘I’ is preferred over ‘me’, because the former acts as a subject, while the latter is used as an object. The position of pronoun is: 2nd person + 3rd person + 1st person. So, it is Anshita and I.

q: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. despise
sol: Flamboyant is marked by or given to strikingly elaborate or colourful display or behaviour. A strident sound is loud, unpleasant, and rough. The meaning of understated is avoiding obvious emphasis or embellishment. Thus, ‘understated ‘ is the antonym of ‘flamboyant’.

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. “I have been exercising for many years,” claimed Parvathi.
opts: Parvathi claimed that she is exercising for many years
Parvathi claimed that she had been exercising for many years
Parvathi claimed that she had exercised for many years.
Parvathi claimed that she has been exercising for many years.
sol: The verb ‘will appreciate’ will change to past form ‘would appreciate’; ‘I’ will change to ‘he’ and ‘your’ changes to ‘their’. The correct sentence is: The new captain informed the team that he would appreciate their support.

q: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence. “When will my parcel arrive?” Madhav asked.
opts: Madhav asked when his parcel would be arriving
Madhav asked when his parcel would arrive
Madhav asked when his parcel arrived
Madhav asked when his parcel will arrive
sol: In the sentence “When will you complete your project?” my teacher asked, the reporting verb changes to asked and ‘will’ changes to ‘would’. The format followed in interrogative sentences is: question word + noun/pronoun + verb + object. My teacher asked when I would complete my project.

q: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom get down to brass tacks
opts: to do whatever it takes to achieve something
to stand at the lowest step or rung of a ladder
to start talking about the basic facts of a situation
to tell someone only what they need to know.
sol: The statement ‘Lord Carnarvon, was a rich man interested in archaeology and who generously spent his money on excavations’ reinstates the statement ‘Lord Carnarvon sponsored excavations because he loved archaeology.’ Option B and C are contradictory, while A is incorrect.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 1.
sol: The sentence ‘In fact, we would never have heard of him if not for the magnificent treasures found in his tomb’ evinces that Tutenkhamen is remembered today because his tomb contained treasures.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 2.
sol: When the last man climbed out of the tomb, a sudden sandstorm blew up and people saw a hawk; locals took this to mean that the spirit of the dead king had left his tomb, cursing those who had opened it. This clarifies that the sight of the hawk flying in the skies suggested that the king’s spirit had escaped from the tomb.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 3.
sol: The tomb of Tutenkhamen was discovered in 1922 AD, because from 1917 to 1922, Howard Carter and Lord Camarvon had been excavating in the Valley of the Kings without finding anything new. It was in 1922 that the tomb of Tutenkhamen was discovered.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 4.
sol: You have watched that film before, haven’t you? The question tag ‘haven’t you’ will be used because the sentence has ‘have’ form. It is to be noted that when the sentence is in affirmative, the question tag is negative.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 5. features 2. aspects 3. characteristic 4. quality Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 6.
sol: The idiom ‘wash one’s hands off something’ means ‘to refuse to acknowledge as one’s own or as one’s responsibility’. Example: When he understood he will be held up for his reckless driving, he tried to wash his hands off.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 7.
sol: Scrumptious implies ‘extremely tasty; delicious’.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 8.
sol: Raucous is a disturbingly harsh and loud noise. So the antonym is ‘subdued’.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 9.
sol: ‘Pull up’ means to stop something; ‘pull out’ is to withdraw. Even if our main players pull out, we will go ahead with the match as scheduled.

q: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank no 10.
sol: My sister was angry with me because I damaged her stapler. Other three options are applicable to soft items, not hard objects.
